quizTitle: AI in Banking & Financial Services – Batch A

questions.question: In the concept-stack slide, which layer is described as 'predicts & classifies'?
Which layer is described as 'plans & acts'?
On the maturity curve slide, which period is labeled 'Predictive AI'?
According to the deck, what percentage of finance teams will use agentic AI in 2026?
Which bank has all 60,000+ employees on Microsoft Copilot Chat plus an internal LLM?
What is 'BarxBot'?
options: A live Barclays Markets tool that helps traders initiate FX trades faster

questions.question: Which UK bank has called 2026 'the year of Agentic AI'?
In insurance, what does the deck say should stay scoped to low-risk steps, with humans retained on consequential decisions?
What does the FCA stand for, as spelled out in the deck?
What is the FCA's stated approach to intervening in firms' AI use?
options: Pre-approval required for every AI model before launch
Zero tolerance for any AI error
Principles-based, outcomes-focused, intervening mainly on 'egregious failures that are not dealt with'
No involvement until a formal complaint is filed

questions.question: Who did NatWest Group's India GCC partner with in April 2026, per the deck's source citation?
options: The Bank of England Innovation Hub

questions.question: According to the 'Bigger Picture' slide, what is changing about India's GCCs?
options: They are shutting down in favour of fully remote teams
They are moving from executing well-defined, lower-risk tasks to co-owning AI/data problems
They are being replaced by European tech hubs
They now only handle payroll and HR functions
